name: Intrinio API
what_it_does: Provides institutional-grade financial market data including real-time stock prices, company fundamentals, options chains, economic indicators, and news via REST API and WebSocket.
use_cases: Build stock screeners that filter equities by fundamental ratios and technical indicators
Power portfolio analysis agents that monitor real-time price moves and earnings surprises
Create earnings-monitoring workflows that trigger alerts when EPS beats or misses consensus
Fetch options chains and Greeks for derivatives pricing and risk analysis agents
Integrate economic data feeds (CPI, unemployment, Fed rates) into macro-aware trading systems
not_for: Crypto or FX traders needing non-equity asset classes as primary data source
Projects requiring zero-latency tick data below 500ms without enterprise contracts
best_when: Best when you need broad, high-quality US equity and options data with reliable uptime and multiple SDK languages for rapid agent integration.
avoid_when: Avoid when budget is constrained and free-tier historical depth (limited quarters) is insufficient for your backtesting or fundamental analysis needs.

auth.notes: Single API key passed as query parameter or Authorization header. Separate sandbox and production keys available.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Limited historical data (recent quarters only), 50 requests/minute, select data feeds only
pricing.paid_tiers: Individual plans from $50/month; professional and enterprise plans with full historical depth and higher rate limits available on request
pricing.notes: 400+ data feeds available; pricing varies per feed. Some premium feeds (e.g., institutional-grade options) require separate subscription.

performance.rate_limits: 50 requests/minute on free tier; 500 requests/minute on paid plans; WebSocket available for real-time streaming

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: HTTP status codes are standard; error bodies include a message field but rarely include actionable remediation hints.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All endpoints are read-only GET requests; fully idempotent by design.
known_agent_gotchas: WebSocket feed requires separate authentication handshake not covered by the standard API key flow
Some data feeds return null fields for non-US securities without clearly flagging the gap
Rate limit headers are present but documented inconsistently across endpoint reference pages

security_score_components.security_notes: API key passed as query param is an antipattern; header-based auth is supported and preferred. No per-key scope restrictions available.
